ogTitle: Drysdale River vs Dudawa: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Drysdale River and Dudawa.
heading: Drysdale River vs Dudawa — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Drysdale River, Western Australia and Dudawa, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Drysdale River has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Dudawa (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Drysdale River or Dudawa safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Drysdale River has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Dudawa scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Drysdale River scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Dudawa scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Drysdale River or Dudawa?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Drysdale River has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
